idea-plugin.name: ApiHelper
idea-plugin.description:
ApiHelper

A lightweight API assistant for Spring MVC, Spring Cloud OpenFeign, and Spring 6 @HttpExchange, built for endpoint navigation, endpoint browsing, and API debugging directly inside IntelliJ IDEA.

Features

  • Bidirectional gutter navigation between @FeignClient / @HttpExchange interfaces and @RestController methods.
  • Controller gutter actions — right-click Controller gutter arrows to debug the endpoint or copy the resolved URL.
  • Endpoint explorer — browse Controller and Feign / HttpExchange endpoints from the ApiHelper tool window.
  • API debugging — send HTTP requests with query, path, header, cookie, and body parameters.
  • Body modes — supports none, form-data, x-www-form-urlencoded, JSON, XML, raw, and binary requests.
  • Spring configuration aware — automatically resolves server.servlet.context-path, spring.mvc.servlet.path, application-{profile}.yml and ${var:default} placeholders.
  • Java & Kotlin sources, courtesy of UAST.
  • Project-level caching with incremental refresh on PSI and Spring configuration changes.
  • Bilingual UI — English & Simplified Chinese bundle.

Compatibility

IntelliJ IDEA 2024.3+ (build 243+). Requires the bundled Java and Kotlin plugins.

Links

  • Source on GitHub
  • Changelog
  • Report an issue
idea-plugin.change-notes:
1.0.1

  • Removed debugger entry from Feign / HttpExchange endpoint list context menus.
  • Automatically prefill Path, Query, Header, Cookie, and JSON Body draft parameters when opening the debugger from an endpoint.
  • Controller gutter context menus now support both debugging the endpoint and copying the resolved URL.
  • Feign / HttpExchange gutter icons keep left-click navigation only, with no context-menu actions.

1.0.0

  • Initial ApiHelper release.
  • Added Spring MVC Controller, OpenFeign, and @HttpExchange endpoint navigation.
  • Added endpoint explorer and lightweight API debugger in the ApiHelper tool window.
  • Added Query, Path, Header, Cookie, form-data, x-www-form-urlencoded, JSON, XML, raw, and binary request support.
  • Added Spring configuration resolution for context path, servlet path, profile, server port, and placeholders.
  • Added Java and Kotlin source support via UAST.
  • English & Simplified Chinese bundle.
